DISCOVERY, 302, MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IN NOTICE OF DEPOSITION FOR JAKE REIS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S CODE COMPLAINT RESPONSE DATED APRIL 25, 2016 ADVISING THE CELL PHONE RECORDS AS WELL AS THE CELL PHONE IS NO LONGER IN HIS POSSESSION IS DISPOSITIVE OF THE ISSUE. THAT THE DEFENDANT WAS SPEAKING OR TEXTING ON HIS CELL PHONE, ALTHOUGH DENIED AT DEPOSITION, IS A FACTOR THAT MAY BE FURTHER EXPLORED THROUGH DISCOVERY. DEFENDANT AGREES CELL PHONE RECORDS MAY PROPERLY BE OBTAINABLE FROM DEFENDANT'S CELL PHONE PROVIDER COMPANY THROUGH A SUBPOENA. THE DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE THE PLAINTIFF WITH AN AUTHORIZATION PERMITTING THE CELL PHONE PROVIDER COMPANY, ON RECEIPT OF A FORMAL SUBPOENA, TO FORWARD THE RECORDS FOR THE ACCIDENT DATE SOLELY TO THE ATTORNEY FOR THE DEFENDANT. THE COURT WILL THEN UNDERTAKE AN IN-CAMERA REVIEW OF THE CELL PHONE RECORDS TO DETERMINE IF THEY INDICATED THE PHONE WAS IN USE IN THE TIME-FRAME OF THE SUBJECT ACCIDENT. BOTH PARTIES' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jul-12-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff Response To Request For Production Of Documents In Notice Of Deposition For Jake Reis And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-24-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Susan Y. Kang Gordon, of Law Office of Susan Kang Gordon, Tel. 510.400.6146, for plaintiffs;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. PLAINTIFF PYONG CHIN Motion To Compel Plaintiff Response To Request For Production Of Documents In Notice Of Deposition For Jake Reis is denied. Defendant's code complaint response dated April 25, 2016 advising the cell phone records as well as the cell phone is no longer in his possession is dispositive of the issue. That the defendant was speaking or texting on his cell phone, although denied at deposition, is a factor that may be further explored through discovery. Defendant agrees cell phone records may properly be obtainable from defendant's cell phone provider company through a subpoena. The defendant shall provide the plaintiff with an authorization permitting the cell phone provider company, on receipt of a formal subpoena, to forward the records for the accident date solely to the attorney for the defendant. The Court will then undertake an in-camera review of the cell phone records to determine if they indicated the phone was in use in the time-frame of the subject accident. Both parties' request for sanctions is denied. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

Susan Kang Gordon, of the Law Offices of Susan Kang Gordon, Tel. 510.400.6146, for plaintiffs;
Brian Leach, of the Law Offices of Leach & McGreevy, LLP, Tel. 415.775.4455 ext. 309, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. DEFENDANT R&S ERECTION, INC., JAKE REIS Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Request For Production Of Documents is granted. Plaintiff shall serve verified responses to Defendants' Request for Production of Documents, Set One, in substantial compliance with C.C.P. 2031.230, without objections and produce documents by March 30, 2016. The defendants' request for monetary sanctions is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Charles Ragan; Clerk Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
